crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The hook return property 'user' was renamed to 'currentUser' (line 55 of the new hunk), but ProfileMenu.tsx (src/components/ProfileMenu.tsx) destructures { user } from useAuth(). After this change, 'user' will be undefined, so user.avatarUrl and user.name will throw TypeError at runtime. The null-check 'if (!user)' will also always be true, permanently showing the LoginButton even for authenticated users.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
user: state.currentUser, // backwards compat alias for ProfileMenu.tsx
    currentUser: state.currentUser,
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed user->currentUser breaks ProfileMenu which destructures user from useAuth
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'login' function was renamed to 'signIn' (line 58), but LoginForm.tsx (src/components/LoginForm.tsx) destructures { login } from useAuth() and calls 'await login(email, password)'. After this change, 'login' is undefined, so the form submission handler will throw TypeError: login is not a function, completely breaking the login flow for all users.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
login: signIn, // backwards compat alias for LoginForm.tsx
    signIn,
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed login->signIn breaks LoginForm which calls login(email, password)
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'isAuthenticated' property was renamed to 'isLoggedIn' (line 57), but ProtectedRoute.tsx (src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx) destructures { isAuthenticated } from useAuth(). Since 'isAuthenticated' is now undefined (falsy), the route guard will always redirect to /login, locking out all authenticated users from every protected page in the application.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
isAuthenticated: state.isLoggedIn, // backwards compat alias for ProtectedRoute.tsx
    isLoggedIn: state.isLoggedIn,
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed isAuthenticated->isLoggedIn breaks ProtectedRoute which checks isAuthenticated
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'logout' function was renamed to 'signOut' (line 59), but ProfileMenu.tsx (src/components/ProfileMenu.tsx) destructures { logout } and passes it to the sign-out button's onClick handler. Since 'logout' is now undefined, clicking 'Sign out' calls undefined(), which throws a TypeError. Additionally, UserGreeting.tsx (src/components/UserGreeting.tsx) destructures { user, isAuthenticated } -- both renamed properties -- so the greeting component never renders (isAuthenticated is undefined/falsy) and would show blank text if it did (user is undefined).
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
logout: signOut, // backwards compat alias for ProfileMenu.tsx
    signOut,
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed logout->signOut breaks ProfileMenu sign-out button and UserGreeting display
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies user->currentUser rename breaking ProfileMenu.tsx with specific file path, property access, and runtime impact.
Correctly identifies login->signIn rename breaking LoginForm.tsx with specific file path and TypeError on form submit.
Correctly identifies isAuthenticated->isLoggedIn rename breaking ProtectedRoute.tsx with specific file path and redirect impact.
Correctly identifies logout->signOut rename breaking ProfileMenu.tsx and UserGreeting.tsx with specific file paths and multiple impacts.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering Button to verify UI impact
Find components rendering Button to verify UI impact
Find components rendering Button to verify UI impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'onClick' prop was renamed to 'onPress' (line 11 of the new hunk), but DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx (src/components/DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx) passes 'onClick={onCancel}' and 'onClick={onConfirm}' to its Button components. Since Button now only reads 'onPress', these handlers are silently ignored -- both the Cancel and Delete buttons render but do absolutely nothing when clicked, making the dialog impossible to dismiss or confirm.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
onClick?: () => void; // keep backwards compat for DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx
  onPress?: () => void;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed onClick->onPress breaks DeleteConfirmDialog cancel and delete buttons
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'variant' prop was renamed to 'appearance' (line 9) and its valid values changed from 'primary'|'secondary'|'danger'|'ghost' to 'filled'|'outlined'|'ghost'|'link'. ToolbarActions.tsx (src/components/ToolbarActions.tsx) passes variant="secondary" and variant="ghost", CheckoutPage.tsx (src/pages/CheckoutPage.tsx) passes variant="primary", and DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx (src/components/DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx) passes variant="danger". Since Button reads 'appearance' (defaulting to 'filled'), the 'variant' prop is ignored in all three files. Every button gets the 'btn-filled' CSS class instead of its intended style.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
variant?: ButtonAppearance; // backwards compat alias
  appearance?: ButtonAppearance;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed variant->appearance breaks styling in ToolbarActions, CheckoutPage, and DeleteConfirmDialog
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'size' prop values changed from 'small'|'medium'|'large' to 'xs'|'sm'|'md'|'lg'|'xl' (line 5 and 10). ToolbarActions.tsx (src/components/ToolbarActions.tsx) passes size="small" and CheckoutPage.tsx (src/pages/CheckoutPage.tsx) passes size="large". These produce CSS classes 'btn-small' and 'btn-large' instead of 'btn-sm' and 'btn-lg', resulting in unstyled or incorrectly sized buttons. At the TypeScript level this is a type error, and at runtime the wrong class names are silently applied.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode: size?: ButtonSize | 'small' | 'medium' | 'large'; // accept legacy values
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Changed size values from small/medium/large to xs/sm/md/lg/xl breaks ToolbarActions and CheckoutPage button sizing
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: ToolbarActions.tsx (src/components/ToolbarActions.tsx) passes 'onClick={onAdd}', 'onClick={onExport}', and 'onClick={onFilter}' to three separate Button instances. Since Button now only reads 'onPress' (line 11), none of these toolbar action buttons will fire their handlers when clicked. The Add, Export, and Filter buttons will all be visually rendered but completely non-functional.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: ToolbarActions passes onClick to Button which now only reads onPress, making Add/Export/Filter buttons non-functional
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies onClick->onPress rename breaking DeleteConfirmDialog with specific file path and user-facing impact.
Correctly identifies variant->appearance rename breaking three consumer files with specific file paths and CSS class impact.
Correctly identifies size enum value changes breaking ToolbarActions and CheckoutPage with specific file paths.
Correctly identifies onClick->onPress rename specifically breaking ToolbarActions toolbar buttons with specific file path.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering selectUserProfile to verify UI impact
Find components rendering selectItems to verify UI impact
Find components rendering selectUserSettings to verify UI impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The selector 'selectUserProfile' was renamed to 'selectProfile' (line 88 of the new hunk), but WorkspaceHeader.tsx (src/components/WorkspaceHeader.tsx) imports and calls useSelector(selectUserProfile). This will be a compile-time error since the named export no longer exists, or at runtime in plain JS, selectUserProfile will be undefined and useSelector(undefined) will throw, crashing the entire header component.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: export const selectProfile = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.profile;
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
export const selectProfile = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.profile;
export const selectUserProfile = selectProfile; // backwards compat for WorkspaceHeader.tsx
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed selectUserProfile->selectProfile breaks WorkspaceHeader import
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The selector 'selectUserSettings' was renamed to 'selectPreferences' (line 89), and the state shape changed from nested 'state.workspace.user.settings' to flat 'state.workspace.preferences'. Both WorkspaceHeader.tsx (src/components/WorkspaceHeader.tsx) and SettingsPanel.tsx (src/components/SettingsPanel.tsx) import selectUserSettings, which no longer exists. Both components will crash at the import/module level, breaking the settings panel and the theme indicator in the header.
suggestionsToEvaluate.existingCode: export const selectPreferences = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.preferences;
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
export const selectPreferences = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.preferences;
export const selectUserSettings = selectPreferences; // backwards compat for WorkspaceHeader.tsx, SettingsPanel.tsx
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed selectUserSettings->selectPreferences breaks WorkspaceHeader and SettingsPanel imports
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The selector 'selectItems' was renamed to 'selectAllItems' (line 90) and the state changed from a flat array 'state.workspace.items' to a normalized map 'itemsById' with Object.values() conversion. ItemList.tsx (src/components/ItemList.tsx) and ItemDetail.tsx (src/components/ItemDetail.tsx) both import selectItems which no longer exists. Both components will fail at import time -- ItemList cannot render the item list, and ItemDetail cannot find related items for the detail view.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed selectItems->selectAllItems breaks ItemList and ItemDetail imports
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The state field 'itemCount' was removed from the state interface (old line 40) in favor of a computed selector derived from selectAllItems (line 94). While the selector name 'selectItemCount' survived, ItemList.tsx (src/components/ItemList.tsx) imports it alongside the now-missing 'selectItems'. Since the import statement 'import { selectItems, selectItemCount }' will fail because selectItems is not exported, the itemCount selector becomes unreachable as well. Additionally, any SSR hydration relying on itemCount in the serialized state will find the field missing, causing a hydration mismatch.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
export const selectItemCount = createSelector(selectAllItems, (items) => items.length);
// Note: removed itemCount state field - now computed from itemsById
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Removed itemCount state field and broken selectItems import makes ItemList component crash entirely
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies selectUserProfile->selectProfile rename breaking WorkspaceHeader with specific file path and crash impact.
Correctly identifies selectUserSettings->selectPreferences rename breaking WorkspaceHeader and SettingsPanel with specific file paths.
Correctly identifies selectItems->selectAllItems rename breaking ItemList and ItemDetail with specific file paths.
Correctly identifies the cascading effect of removed itemCount field and broken selectItems import on ItemList.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering useFormValidation to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useFormValidation to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useFormValidation to verify UI impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The hook signature changed from positional arguments 'useFormValidation(rules, validateOnChange, validateOnBlur)' to an options object 'useFormValidation({ rules, validateOnChange, validateOnBlur })' (lines 41-42). RegistrationForm.tsx (src/components/RegistrationForm.tsx) calls 'useFormValidation(registrationRules)' passing the FieldRules object directly as the options parameter. The hook will try to destructure 'rules' from the FieldRules object, which will be undefined, causing all validation to silently fail. ContactForm.tsx (src/components/ContactForm.tsx) calls 'useFormValidation(contactRules, true, true)' and ProfileEditForm.tsx (src/components/ProfileEditForm.tsx) calls 'useFormValidation(profileRules, false)' -- both pass positional args that will be silently ignored.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
export function useFormValidation(rulesOrOptions: FieldRules | UseFormValidationOptions, validateOnChange = true, validateOnBlur = true): UseFormValidationReturn {
  const options = 'rules' in rulesOrOptions ? rulesOrOptions : { rules: rulesOrOptions, validateOnChange, validateOnBlur };
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Changed from positional args to options object breaks all three form components calling with positional rules argument
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The return property names were changed: 'errors' -> 'fieldErrors', 'valid' -> 'isValid', 'dirty' -> 'isDirty' (lines 30-32). All three consumer forms destructure the old names. RegistrationForm.tsx (src/components/RegistrationForm.tsx) reads '{ errors, valid, dirty }' -- all will be undefined. The '!valid' check evaluates to '!undefined' which is true, so the submit button is permanently disabled. ContactForm.tsx (src/components/ContactForm.tsx) reads '{ errors, valid }' with the same breakage -- 'errors.name?.length' will throw TypeError because undefined has no property 'name'.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed errors->fieldErrors, valid->isValid, dirty->isDirty breaks all three forms that destructure old names
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The utility methods were renamed: 'reset' -> 'resetValidation', 'touch' -> 'touchField', 'isTouched' -> 'isFieldTouched', 'firstError' -> 'getFieldError' (lines 35-38). ContactForm.tsx (src/components/ContactForm.tsx) calls 'touch(field)', 'isTouched("name")', and 'reset()' which are all now undefined. Calling 'touch(field)' will throw TypeError. RegistrationForm.tsx (src/components/RegistrationForm.tsx) and ProfileEditForm.tsx (src/components/ProfileEditForm.tsx) call 'firstError("email")' and 'reset()' which are also undefined, crashing on invocation.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed reset/touch/isTouched/firstError to resetValidation/touchField/isFieldTouched/getFieldError breaks all form consumers
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The isFieldTouched callback (line 91-93) has a stale closure over state.touchedFields. Because it captures the Set reference at creation time via useCallback with [state.touchedFields] as the dependency, and the Set is replaced on every touchField call (via 'new Set(prev.touchedFields).add(field)'), the callback properly recreates. However, if a consumer memoizes the callback reference (e.g., passing it as a dep to useEffect), the stale check will miss intermediate touches. Consider exposing a ref-based approach or using useSyncExternalStore for more reliable touched-field tracking.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: isFieldTouched uses useCallback with Set dependency which may cause stale closure issues in memoized consumers
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies positional-to-options signature change breaking all three form components with specific file paths and runtime impact.
Correctly identifies return field renames breaking all three forms with specific file paths, TypeError details, and UI impact.
Correctly identifies method renames breaking all three consumer forms with specific file paths and TypeError on invocation.
Valid observation about stale closure risk with useCallback and Set dependency in touched-field tracking.

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering useTheme to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useTheme to verify UI impact

Find components rendering useTheme to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useTheme to verify UI impact

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'colors.primary' token was renamed to 'colors.brand' (line 6 of the new hunk), along with 'primaryHover' -> 'brandHover' and 'primaryLight' -> 'brandLight' (lines 7-8). PrimaryButton.tsx (src/components/PrimaryButton.tsx) uses 'theme.colors.primary' for its backgroundColor, which is now undefined. The button will render with no background color (transparent), making it invisible or unreadable against the page background.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed colors.primary->brand breaks PrimaryButton background color rendering
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The spacing tokens were renamed from short names ('xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg', 'xl') to full names ('xsmall', 'small', 'medium', 'large', 'xlarge') on lines 19-23. PrimaryButton.tsx (src/components/PrimaryButton.tsx) uses 'theme.spacing.sm' and 'theme.spacing.md' for padding. Card.tsx (src/components/Card.tsx) uses 'theme.spacing.lg'. DropdownMenu.tsx (src/components/DropdownMenu.tsx) uses 'theme.spacing.xs' and 'theme.spacing.sm'. FormField.tsx (src/components/FormField.tsx) uses 'theme.spacing.md' and 'theme.spacing.xs'. All these references return undefined, resulting in collapsed spacing across 5 components.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed spacing tokens xs/sm/md/lg/xl to xsmall/small/medium/large/xlarge breaks 5+ components
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'breakpoints' type changed from a named object '{ mobile, tablet, desktop, wide }' to a positional tuple '[string, string, string, string]' (line 25). ResponsiveGrid.tsx (src/components/ResponsiveGrid.tsx) accesses 'theme.breakpoints.tablet' and 'theme.breakpoints.desktop' for CSS media queries. Since breakpoints is now an array, '.tablet' and '.desktop' return undefined, producing '@media (min-width: undefined)' which is an invalid media query. The grid will never switch to multi-column layout on wider screens.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode: breakpoints: { mobile: string; tablet: string; desktop: string; wide: string };
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Changed breakpoints from named object to array breaks ResponsiveGrid media queries
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The 'fonts.body' token was renamed to 'fonts.sans' (line 27). PrimaryButton.tsx (src/components/PrimaryButton.tsx), DropdownMenu.tsx (src/components/DropdownMenu.tsx), and FormField.tsx (src/components/FormField.tsx) all use 'theme.fonts.body' for fontFamily. Since 'fonts.body' is now undefined, 'fontFamily: undefined' is set, which causes the browser to fall back to its default serif font (typically Times New Roman), creating a visually jarring inconsistency where some components show the intended sans-serif font and others show serif.
suggestionsToEvaluate.improvedCode:
body: string; // backwards compat alias for PrimaryButton, DropdownMenu, FormField
    sans: string;
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed fonts.body->fonts.sans breaks font rendering in PrimaryButton, DropdownMenu, and FormField
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

suggestionsToEvaluate.suggestionContent: The shadow tokens were renamed: 'shadows.card' -> 'shadows.elevation1', 'shadows.dropdown' -> 'shadows.elevation2', 'shadows.sm' -> 'shadows.subtle' (lines 32-35). Card.tsx (src/components/Card.tsx) uses 'theme.shadows.card' for its box shadow, which is now undefined -- cards render flat with no shadow. DropdownMenu.tsx (src/components/DropdownMenu.tsx) uses 'theme.shadows.dropdown' which is also undefined, so dropdown menus appear without any shadow or visual separation from the background, making them hard to distinguish from the page content.
suggestionsToEvaluate.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed shadows.card/dropdown/sm to elevation1/elevation2/subtle breaks Card and DropdownMenu box shadows
suggestionsToEvaluate.label: bug

expectedActions.reason: Correctly identifies colors.primary->brand rename breaking PrimaryButton with specific file path and visual impact.
Correctly identifies spacing token renames breaking 5 consumer components with specific file paths and collapsed layout impact.
Correctly identifies breakpoints type change from named object to array breaking ResponsiveGrid media queries with specific file path.
Correctly identifies fonts.body->sans rename breaking 3 consumer components with specific file paths and font fallback impact.
Correctly identifies shadow token renames breaking Card and DropdownMenu with specific file paths and visual impact.
